# Embracing innovation, VA makes advancements in health care

![A VA physician standing in front of computer screens, demonstrating our embrace of technology and innovation.](https://vacareers.va.gov/wp-content/uploads/sites/5/innovation-header.jpg?w=1024)

[Innovation](https://vacareers.va.gov/career-guidance/innovative-careers/) has long been a cornerstone of the work we do at VA, from developing the first implantable cardiac pacemaker in 1960 and the first electronic health record in 1970, to being one of the first U.S. health care systems to adopt 5G and test its use cases in 2020.

Our [Office of Healthcare Innovation and Learning](https://www.innovation.va.gov/) (OHIL) is dedicated to supporting and developing innovative health care solutions for Veterans. By embracing cutting-edge technologies and fostering a culture of continuous improvement, we’re finding new and exciting ways to meet the needs of our Veterans.

With the theme of “From Promise to Progress: Evolving Veteran Healthcare,” our annual [State of Innovation Report](https://www.innovation.va.gov/hil/home.html) highlights some of the innovative projects we’ve undertaken this year. Through these innovations, we encourage our employees to test assumptions and reinvent care models, allowing us to better reach the varied populations of Veterans that we serve.

Here are just a few of those projects, all spearheaded by VA employees.

## **Reaching rural Veterans**

Since only 9-12% of Veterans follow up on their doctors’ orders to see a physical therapist, our Office of Rehabilitation and Prosthetic Services has developed a proactive and personalized approach to physical therapy for Veterans. Through the VA [Physical Therapy Patient Aligned Care Team](https://marketplace.va.gov/innovations/pt-embedded-within-pact?utm_source=Online+2024+SOI&utm_medium=Online+2024+SOI&utm_id=Online+2024+VHA+State+of+Innovation+Report) (PACT PT), we’re embedding physical therapists into the primary care team processes, providing Veterans access to same-day care for musculoskeletal or neurological issues.

Gaps in podiatric care for feet can lead to discomfort and, in extreme cases, increased risk of limb amputation, but regular in-person care can be burdensome for rural Veterans. To meet this need, the [High-Risk Eye and Limb Preservation Program](https://marketplace.va.gov/innovations/high-risk-eye-and-limb-preservation-program-helpp?utm_source=Online+2024+SOI&utm_medium=Online+2024+SOI&utm_id=Online+2024+VHA+State+of+Innovation+Report) (HELPP) integrates former military medics who now work as intermediate care technicians (ICTs) into podiatric visits. HELPP’s teamwork between virtual podiatrists and in-person [ICTs](https://vacareers.va.gov/career-guidance/veterans-and-transitioning-military/#intermediate-care-technician-program) has helped many Veterans receive crucial care.

## **Exploring virtual reality**

Concerned that many Veterans are experiencing social isolation, VA Immersive implemented the [Peer Social Support](https://www.innovation.va.gov/hil/views/immersive/immersive.html?utm_source=Online+2024+SOI&utm_medium=Online+2024+SOI&utm_id=Online+2024+VHA+State+of+Innovation+Report) pilot. This program provides access to virtual reality (VR) platforms that offer opportunities to cultivate camaraderie and connection. The program also provides training for VA providers who are offering VR headsets to Veterans, focusing on effectively supporting the use of the virtual platform.

[Virtual Reality-Based At-Home Chronic Pain Management](https://www.innovation.va.gov/hil/assets/documents/va-immersive-introduction-guide-summer-2023-public.pdf) provides Veterans with the opportunity to learn different pain management techniques activities like deep breathing exercises, relaxation, pain education, or mindfulness-based practices. This technology enables Veterans to learn how to independently manage their pain through structured sessions that can decrease levels of reliance on pharmaceuticals, all from the comfort of their homes.

## **Advancements in science**

For Veterans, the benefits of [pharmacogenomics](http://www.cancer.va.gov/oncology-services-and-tools.html) (PGx)—analyzing a blood sample to determine how an individual’s genes affect response to medications—can be profound. By integrating PGx information into a Veteran’s care, our clinicians can use it to provide medications that are better tailored to their genetic makeup. PGx minimizes adverse effects and toxicity, and translates to prescriptions that are no longer one-size-fits-all.

By harnessing augmented reality (AR) and artificial intelligence to advance medical imaging, VA has expanded our ability to deliver clinical care with the use of [wireless ultrasound technology](https://www.innovation.va.gov/ecosystem/views/national-centers/ncchi/?utm_source=Online+2024+SOI&utm_medium=Online+2024+SOI&utm_id=Online+2024+VHA+State+of+Innovation+Report). This technology has improved precision in procedures, allowing for exact targeting and error reduction, which ultimately improves the quality of care for Veterans. This innovation is the result of work done by our National Center for Collaborative Healthcare Innovation (NCCHI).

### **Work at VA**

Our people are the most essential ingredient for the change we hope to achieve together. Join our team and be part of an innovative culture of care.

--- SOURCE 2: Understanding VA Health Innovations for Military Veterans - KDVMA ---

SUMMARY:
The VA is dedicated to providing innovative healthcare and tech solutions. They aim to meet veterans' unique health needs. Key Innovations

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://www.kdvma.com/frontend/images/logo.png)

# VA Health Care Innovations for Military Veterans – Breaking New Ground in Veteran Care

### VA Health Care Innovations for Military Veterans – Breaking New Ground in Veteran Care

![](../uploads/blog//woman-soldier-tells-a-military-doctor-about-her-he-2024-10-20-21-51-56-utc.jpeg)

![](../uploads/blog//woman-soldier-tells-a-military-doctor-about-her-he-2024-10-20-21-51-56-utc.jpeg)

**Table of Content**

**Summary**

This blog showcases healthcare innovations for veterans. They are transformative. The discussion presents all the advantages, obstacles, and important advancements for care delivery. Blackbody designs its content for better access. It aims to use digital healthcare to improve veteran care. It seeks to combine personalized treatment with this technology.

## **Introduction**

The U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) improves services to veterans nationwide. Innovations aim to improve healthcare. They seek to make it more accessible and use modern technology. The VA is dedicated to providing innovative healthcare and tech solutions. They aim to meet veterans' unique health needs.

## **Key Innovations in VA Health Care**

The VA is developing new healthcare apps. They aim to improve veterans' access to services and enhance their well-being.

Telehealth has become an essential infrastructure that supports VA healthcare operations. The VA's remote healthcare consultations provide vital care to veterans in rural areas. Telehealth allows for remote visits. This reduces the need for in-person meetings. It streamlines health services and cuts costs without losing care.

The VA system uses artificial intelligence to drive a transformation in diagnostic capabilities. AI tools help medical providers. They can quickly detect health problems. Then, they can design targeted treatments that improve patient outcomes.

VA.gov has deployed state-of-the-art prosthetic devices that exhibit upgraded operational abilities. Veterans with physical disabilities now receive custom medical equipment. It improves their mobility and comfort. This helps them live independently and actively.

The provision of mental health services continues to receive maximum priority. Veterans who use VA mental health apps benefit from virtual and AI therapy. All these services focus on treating PTSD and issues, including depression and anxiety.

The VA's advanced electronic health record systems aim to improve care coordination. The systems allow easy access to medical histories. This boosts treatment efficiency and reduces errors among healthcare providers.

## **Pros and Cons of VA Health Care Innovations**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **PROS** | **CONS** |
| Provides care for veterans in remote areas. | Relies on internet availability in all regions. |
| Enhances early disease detection and treatment precision. | Requires infrastructure and training for medical staff. |
| Restores independence with advanced mobility solutions. |  |
| Offers easy access to mental health resources. |  |
| Improves care coordination and reduces delays. |  |

**PROS**

**CONS**

Provides care for veterans in remote areas.

Relies on internet availability in all regions.

Enhances early disease detection and treatment precision.

Requires infrastructure and training for medical staff.

Restores independence with advanced mobility solutions.

Offers easy access to mental health resources.

Improves care coordination and reduces delays.

## **Impact on Veterans' Lives**

The innovations have brought substantial improvements to veterans' quality of life. By using telehealth, veterans anywhere can maintain continuous access to medical treatment. AI has automated care delivery using electronic records. Enhanced prosthetics and mental health technology now provide personalized treatments. These modern healthcare advances create systems that honor veterans' sacrifices.

## **Challenges and Future Directions**

VA healthcare innovations show promise. But they face issues. They have technical infrastructure and training needs, as well as high costs. VA uses investments alongside technological partnerships with providers to resolve these issues. Veteran healthcare will improve in the future. It will offer better access and personalized care for all veterans.

## **Conclusion**

Healthcare innovations have improved services for military veterans. They now have better access, personalization, and quality. Advanced telehealth and prosthetic advancements are medical solutions for veterans. With sustained investments, the future holds health gains for veterans. They served their nation.

## **Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)**

**What is telehealth, and how does it help veterans?**

Telehealth lets veterans get remote care via video calls with providers. Telehealth video consultations reduce travel needs. They also improve access to essential medical care.

**How does AI improve VA healthcare?**

AI helps diagnose illnesses faster. The system creates tailored care strategies to improve patient outcomes.

**Are VA innovations available to all veterans?**

These services target all eligible veterans in every state. That is their design.

**What is the biggest challenge in implementing VA health care innovations?**

AI can help all veterans. However, high-tech costs and poor rural Internet access limit its use.

**How do mental health apps benefit veterans?**

Mental health apps deliver accessible resources for treating PTSD alongside anxiety. Mobile apps give veterans 24/7 access to mental health help and relaxation training.

--- SOURCE 3: Understanding the Unique Needs of Veterans in Healthcare Facilities ---
URL: https://www.downersgrovehc.com/blog/understanding-the-unique-needs-of-veterans-in-healthcare-facilities

SUMMARY:
To enhance healthcare services for veterans, expanding access to treatment options tailored specifically to their unique needs is essential.

FULL CONTENT:
![downers grove healthcare](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/666c936e83bdb4f7e47f3aec/666c936e83bdb4f7e47f3b22_HVH_logo_horizontal_Downers-2048x254.webp)

# Understanding the Unique Needs of Veterans in Healthcare Facilities

April 8, 2025

Catering to Veteran-Specific Healthcare Needs

![Understanding the Unique Needs of Veterans in Healthcare Facilities  ](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44912cf9ee9f884e064_tmpt9k6xvcd.jpeg)

### The Intersection of Military Experience and Healthcare

Veterans represent a unique subset of the population with complex healthcare needs influenced by their military service. To deliver effective healthcare, it is crucial for practitioners to understand how military experiences affect veterans' mental, physical, and emotional well-being. This narrative delves into various aspects of veteran healthcare, from specific health challenges to systemic barriers and strategies for improvement.

## Understanding Veterans' Unique Healthcare Needs

![A Deeper Insight into Veterans' Healthcare Challenges](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e08f_a5ef97a02bcdc70283c531e29dbfb1939a9c0e3b.png)

![A Deeper Insight into Veterans' Healthcare Challenges](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e08f_a5ef97a02bcdc70283c531e29dbfb1939a9c0e3b.png)

### How can you better understand a veteran's healthcare needs?

To better understand a veteran's healthcare needs, itâs essential to learn about their military background and the specific traumas they may have experienced during service. This knowledge allows healthcare providers to approach treatment with sensitivity and insight.

Veterans often face unique psychological and physical health challenges stemming from their service. Conditions such as PTSD are common and can influence various aspects of healthcare. Therefore, caregivers trained to recognize and respond to mental health issues significantly enhance care delivery.

Additionally, providing a structured routine can help veterans navigate their healthcare journeys, offering them a sense of stability. Promoting independence is crucial, as it empowers veterans and can boost their self-esteem.

Building social connections through support groups or connections with fellow veterans can mitigate feelings of isolation often felt by those transitioning to civilian life. Communication with the veteran's family is also vital, as it ensures a robust support system.

Lastly, coordinating with Veterans Affairs can help guarantee that veterans receive the comprehensive support and benefits they require, addressing their unique healthcare challenges effectively.

### Key Aspects of Veteransâ Healthcare Needs

| **Aspect** | **Description** | **Importance** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Military Background | Understanding service history and trauma experiences | Tailors care for effective treatment |
| Trauma Awareness | Sensitivity to conditions like PTSD and its impacts on health | Ensures compassionate and relevant healthcare |
| Caregiver Training | Education on veteransâ specific needs and issues | Enhances quality of care |
| Structured Routine | Providing stability through predictable healthcare interactions | Builds trust and reduces anxiety |
| Promoting Independence | Encouraging veterans to engage in their own care decisions | Boosts self-esteem and autonomy |
| Family Communication | Keeping families involved in care decisions to foster a comprehensive support system | Strengthens care coordination and support |

Understanding these elements is crucial for improving the quality of care provided to veterans, helping them navigate their unique healthcare landscape effectively.

## Complex Health Challenges Faced by Veterans

![Addressing the Multifaceted Health Issues Veterans Encounter](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0ab_1be58796012cb95279f379e46b38b5441f52ab4c.png)

![Addressing the Multifaceted Health Issues Veterans Encounter](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0ab_1be58796012cb95279f379e46b38b5441f52ab4c.png)

### What are the unique health challenges faced by veterans?

Veterans often contend with a range of unique health challenges that set them apart from the general population. Mental health disorders are notably prevalent, with significant rates of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), depression, and anxiety affecting many. Statistics reveal that veterans are 16% more likely than civilians to experience these conditions, which is exacerbated by the stressors of military service.

### The Impact of Homelessness and Chronic Pain on Veterans

Chronic pain is a common issue observed in veterans, with approximately 82% of those from Operations Enduring Freedom and Iraqi Freedom reporting chronic discomfort. This pain can stem from combat-related injuries and other service-related factors. Homelessness adds another layer of distress, affecting about 50,000 veterans at any given time. Often, this situation ties back to their mental health struggles, as issues like PTSD and depression can leave veterans isolated and without stable housing.

### Understanding Suicide Rates Among Veterans

The suicide rates among veterans are alarmingly high, with 18 to 22 veterans committing suicide daily. This tragic statistic particularly highlights the vulnerability of younger veterans aged 18 to 44. The intersection of mental health issues, chronic pain, and the stigma surrounding seeking help contributes significantly to this crisis. Addressing these urgent challenges requires tailored healthcare strategies, including accessible mental health resources and improved community support systems, to better meet the veterans' specific needs.

## Improving Healthcare Services for Veterans

![Strategies to Enhance Healthcare Services for Veterans](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0a8_98359716b88ccd612143fe7e0be7df33ba7ced0a.png)

![Strategies to Enhance Healthcare Services for Veterans](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0a8_98359716b88ccd612143fe7e0be7df33ba7ced0a.png)

### How can we improve healthcare services for veterans?

To enhance healthcare services for veterans, expanding access to treatment options tailored specifically to their unique needs is essential. This includes addressing mental health challenges like PTSD and chronic conditions arising from military service.

Investing in training and education for healthcare staff improves their understanding of veterans' experiences and cultural nuances. Staff equipped with this knowledge can identify health issues more accurately and coordinate appropriate care, fostering trust with veteran patients.

Engaging veterans' families in the care process strengthens support networks. By respecting their needs and preferences, families can play an active role in the rehabilitation and recovery of veterans.

Healthcare organizations must promote their services effectively within the community. Outreach to veterans' groups and community leaders will raise awareness about available healthcare programs. Initiatives such as the PACT Act and programs from organizations like Northwell Health focus on bridging gaps in veteran healthcare, contributing positively to their overall care.

By addressing these key areasâtreatment access, staff training, and family involvementâhealthcare services for veterans can significantly improve.

## Barriers to Access and Strategies to Overcome Them

![Identifying and Overcoming Barriers in Veterans' Healthcare Access](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0ae_31a58b4ba69bc9871b3eb5b855807868e5b9a729.png)

![Identifying and Overcoming Barriers in Veterans' Healthcare Access](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0ae_31a58b4ba69bc9871b3eb5b855807868e5b9a729.png)

### What are the challenges of veterans' healthcare and how can they be addressed?

Veterans' healthcare encounters multiple challenges that can significantly affect their access to necessary services. One of the foremost issues is limited access to VA facilities. Many veterans reside in rural areas, where geographic barriers hinder their ability to reach healthcare, contributing to a disparity in service utilization. Among the over four million veterans living in these regions, only 21% utilize outpatient care compared to a more significant proportion in urban settings.

Long wait times for appointments further add to the challenges. Many veterans report frustration over scheduling and obtaining timely access to care, particularly for mental health services. Alarmingly, less than 50% of veterans who seek mental health treatment receive the necessary assistance, largely due to complicated navigation of the VA system and a lack of awareness regarding available benefits.

To address these challenges, legislative efforts, such as the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act, have been implemented to enhance access to community care, aiming to reduce wait times and offer veterans more options.

Moreover, the integration of telemedicine presents a potential solution to bridge the gap for those in remote areas, enabling easier access to care, particularly for mental health services. Increased awareness about benefits, streamlined systems, enhanced mental health services, and strong advocacy for community-based healthcare programs are crucial strategies for overcoming the existing barriers veterans face in receiving appropriate healthcare.

## Role of Community Health Organizations

![Community Support: A Lifeline for Veterans Seeking Care](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0a5_c02318db84f23bb0a85bcb80a2d14b81589cebaa.png)

![Community Support: A Lifeline for Veterans Seeking Care](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67e3a44a12cf9ee9f884e0a5_c02318db84f23bb0a85bcb80a2d14b81589cebaa.png)

### Support for veterans seeking non-VA care

Community health organizations play a crucial role in supporting veterans who seek care outside the VA system. With only 30% of veterans relying solely on the VA for healthcare, these organizations must be equipped to cater to the specific needs of veterans, particularly those who have experiences that influence their physical and mental health. Many veterans, nearly 80%, also hold additional insurance like TRICARE or Medicare, making it essential for community health workers to be familiar with these overlaps to ensure veterans receive comprehensive care.

### Collaborative healthcare models

Integrating community resources with VA services is vital. Healthcare providers must understand veterans' unique cultural backgrounds and challenges, from PTSD to chronic pain, to deliver effective care. Models like Patient Aligned Care Teams (PACTs) create a streamlined approach that encourages collaboration among various healthcare providers, enhancing care continuity for veterans transitioning to civilian life.

### Raising community awareness

Increasing awareness of available programs and services is essential for connecting veterans with healthcare organizations. Community awareness campaigns can help reduce stigma associated with mental health issues, encouraging veterans to seek help without fear. By prioritizing veterans' needs and promoting educational programs, community health organizations can foster a supportive environment that facilitates access to necessary healthcare.

## VA Healthcare Benefits and Eligibility

### Overview of VA healthcare services

The Veterans Health Administration (VHA) is the largest integrated healthcare system in the U.S., offering a comprehensive range of services tailored to meet the unique needs of over nine million veterans. These services encompass preventive and primary care, urgent and emergency care, specialty care, and mental health services, specifically aimed at addressing conditions such as PTSD and chronic pain. Rehabilitation services are also included, catering to veterans transitioning from military to civilian life through vocational, psychological, and physical therapies.

### Eligibility criteria for VA benefits

To qualify for VA healthcare, veterans must have served in active military duty and received a discharge under conditions other than dishonorable. Eligibility also considers factors like service-connected health issues, income levels, and priority group assignments. Understanding these criteria is crucial for veterans to successfully access essential healthcare benefits.

### Navigating the VA system

While the VA provides extensive healthcare options, many veterans express difficulties in navigating the system. Barriers include complex paperwork, long wait times, and a lack of awareness about available services. To ease navigation, the VA has implemented programs like the Transition Assistance Program, aimed at facilitating access to necessary services for veterans. Additionally, community health workers can play a vital role by providing support and guidance to help veterans effectively utilize their benefits.

## Legislative Measures and Their Impact

### Overview of key legislation affecting veteran healthcare

Legislative measures have significantly influenced veteran healthcare, particularly through acts like the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014 and the MISSION Act of 2018. These laws aim to enhance accessibility, enabling veterans to seek community care and reducing wait times associated with VA facilities. With increased funding directed toward community care, these initiatives recognize the diverse healthcare needs of veterans.

### PACT and MISSION Acts

The Patient Aligned Care Team (PACT) approach emphasizes coordinated care within the VA system, ensuring veterans receive comprehensive health services tailored to their unique needs. The MISSION Act complements this by streamlining access to private healthcare options, allowing veterans to bypass long wait times and increasing patient satisfaction.

### How legislation shapes healthcare experiences

Legislation directly impacts veterans' healthcare experiences by addressing issues such as staffing shortages and resource allocation. For instance, with 91% of VA facilities reporting severe nurse shortages, legislative efforts are crucial to improve conditions and enhance service delivery. Ultimately, effective development and implementation of these legislative measures are vital in shaping a responsive healthcare system for veterans.

## Educational Needs in Healthcare Settings

### Incorporating veteran care into curricula

To effectively address the unique healthcare needs of veterans, educational institutions must integrate veteran care into their curricula. This encompasses understanding the specific physical and mental health challenges faced by veterans, such as PTSD, chronic pain, and moral injury. By fostering this knowledge among healthcare students, future providers can offer more tailored and relevant care to this population.

### Training healthcare providers in military competencies

Healthcare providers need proper training in military competencies and cultural sensitivities to improve interactions with veteran patients. This includes recognizing the impact of military service on health and the significance of a veteran's service history. Training programs should emphasize communication techniques that facilitate trust and rapport, which are essential for effective diagnosis and treatment.

### Impact on treatment outcomes

Enhanced education and training of providers can lead to improved treatment outcomes for veterans. Studies show that when healthcare workers understand the complexities of military culture and the specific health issues relevant to veterans, it significantly influences the quality of care delivered. This, in turn, enhances recovery rates and overall patient satisfaction, directly linking education to better healthcare experiences for veterans.

| Training Aspect | Description | Expected Outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Veteran Care in Curriculum | Integrating veteran-specific health challenges into studentsâ learning | Enhanced understanding among future providers |
| Military Competency Training | Teaching cultural sensitivity and military health impacts | Improved communication & trust |
| Better Treatment Outcomes | Focusing on veterans' unique health issues in care | Higher recovery rates and satisfaction |

## The Aging Veteran Population and Specialized Care

### Unique care requirements for aging veterans

As the veteran population ages, their healthcare needs become increasingly specialized. Aging veterans often face complex health conditions related to their military service, necessitating nuanced and comprehensive care approaches. Chronic pain, mobility issues, and psychological challenges like PTSD are prevalent, requiring healthcare providers to be well-versed in the unique histories and needs of these individuals.

### Long-term care services for elderly veterans

The VA provides a range of long-term care services tailored specifically for elderly veterans. This includes nursing home care, assisted living, and in-home care options to assist with daily living tasks. Such services are crucial in supporting veterans' independence and quality of life, enabling them to age with dignity while receiving necessary medical attention.

### Anticipating future healthcare demand trends

With projections indicating an increasing number of aging veterans, healthcare systems must adapt to meet rising demands. The VA is expected to enhance data collection to better understand these trends and allocate resources effectively. Addressing staffing shortages and ensuring access to specialized care will be crucial for meeting the complex needs of this demographic.

## The Road Ahead for Veteran Healthcare

Addressing the healthcare needs of veterans demands a comprehensive approach grounded in understanding their unique experiences and challenges. As healthcare systems evolve, it is imperative to integrate veteran-specific training and foster community partnerships to fill existing gaps in care. By embracing compassionate and tailored healthcare strategies, and leveraging legislative advancements, we can pave the way for improved health outcomes and quality of life for veterans in healthcare facilities.

## References

![How to Plan for Long-Term Care: A Seniorâs Guide  ](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67f4d2eb8f70bef3b63f65ba_tmpdlgf_d0q.jpeg)

## How to Plan for Long-Term Care: A Seniorâs Guide

![How to support independence in individuals with mobility aids  ](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67f4bf43169e04703258822c_tmpid0myvr8.jpeg)

## How to support independence in individuals with mobility aids

![How Home Health Care Services Can Help Manage Medications  ](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67f4bf372e64ed8042939864_tmpmyv6n9_i.jpeg)

## How Home Health Care Services Can Help Manage Medications

![The Importance of Preventative Care in Aging Populations  ](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/667433ee3e1cef35bc524e53/67f4bec72439caed13ffae96_tmpyafblo9a.jpeg)

## The Importance of Preventative Care in Aging Populations

# Collaborating Across Industries to Improve Veteran Health Care

![Circle lines background](assets/img/drhci/bubble-lines.svg)
![Small group of men and women posing for photo](assets/img/home/nci2i-1.jpg)
![Man from IDEAS talking to woman at conference](assets/img/home/nci2i-2.jpg)
![Indra Sandal with American and VA flags in background](assets/img/home/nci2i-3.jpg)
![Indra Sandal with colleagues](assets/img/home/nci2i-4.jpg)
![Small group of men and women posing for photo at conference](assets/img/home/nci2i-5.jpg)
![Attendees meeting and talking at conference](assets/img/home/nci2i-6.jpg)
![Inventor, Brian Higgins, with the Smart White Cane](assets/img/home/nci2i-7.jpg)
![Small workgroups collaborating at a conference](assets/img/home/nci2i-8.jpg)
![Woman presenting on the stage of iEX 2023](assets/img/home/nci2i-9.jpg)
![iEX 2023 inventors](assets/img/home/nci2i-10.jpg)

## Our Mission

The National Centers for Innovation to Impact (NCi2I) foster collaboration between internal and external problem-solvers and select VA Medical Centers, serving as the cornerstone of a strategic alliance aimed at confronting the VHA's most critical health care challenges. With each center focusing on a distinct domain of expertise, their unified mission is to integrate the VHA's key priorities with transformative, scalable solutions that cater to the needs of Veterans and VHA staff. NCi2I brings this vision to life through a deliberate process of discover, test, replicate, scale. In partnership with Veterans, VA employees, academia, industry, non-profits, and government, NCi2I aims to serve as a convener for all those seeking to tackle the trickiest, most pervasive issues across the VA healthcare landscape.

![NCCHI logo](assets/img/home/ncchi-logo.png)

National Center for Collaborative Healthcare Innovation

NCCHI collaborates throughout VA, across government offices, and with the private and public sector to create a far-reaching positive impact on Veteran care.

[Visit the NCCHI Website](ncchi/)

![NECIE logo](assets/img/home/necie-logo.png)

New England Center for Innovation Excellence

NECIE operationalizes health care innovation by targeting technology solutions for chronic conditions, improving rural access to care, and offering tailored solutions for aging Veterans.

[Visit the NECIE Website](necie/)

![DRHCI logo](assets/img/home/drhci-logo.png)

Digital and Remote Health Center for Innovation

DRHCI acts as a central resource to streamline clinical implementation of digital and remote patient monitoring (RPM) solutions for Veterans.

[Visit the DRHCI Website](drhci/)

![IDEAS logo](assets/img/home/ideas-logo.png)

Innovation, Development, Education, Advanced Implementation, Solutions (IDEAS) Center

IDEAS Center for Innovation empowers frontline employees to leverage their clinical expertise, fosters creative problem-solving, and collaborates with industry and academia to develop solutions that drive health care advancements for Veterans.

[Visit the IDEAS Website](ideas/)

## Accelerating Health Care Innovation

![Surgeon performing surgery](assets/img/home/accelerating-innovation.png)


## Our National Centers

## Featured Successes

### Augmented Reality and 5G

This (collaboration) brings cutting-edge wireless health care solutions, including augmented reality for clinical training, presurgical planning and operative guidance. The partnership (collaboration) includes innovative wireless health care solutions, including 5G wireless connectivity from Verizon, clinical augmented reality software from Medivis, and HoloLens 2 headsets from Microsoft.

![5G cell tower](assets/img/home/5g-tower.png)

## We’re here anytime, day or night - 24/7

If you are a Veteran in crisis or concerned about one, connect with our caring, qualified responders for
confidential help. Many of them are Veterans themselves.

enriched_points.point_title: Importance of Addressing Unique Healthcare Needs of Veterans
enriched_points.expanded_content: Veterans face distinct health challenges stemming from military service, such as PTSD and chronic pain, demanding tailored care approaches that address their specific needs.

enriched_points.point_title: Growing Veteran Population and Demand for Services
enriched_points.expanded_content: The increasing number of veterans, particularly aging populations, heightens the demand for accessible healthcare services, necessitating innovative solutions to meet their diverse requirements.

enriched_points.point_title: Focus on Innovative Solutions for Improved Care
enriched_points.expanded_content: The VA is prioritizing technological advancements like telehealth and AI to enhance care delivery, making healthcare more efficient, accessible, and personalized for veterans.
